rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in the appropriate AADL scope: component type annexes for interface behavior, implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Use assign statements in implementation scope only for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
generic_rules: AGREE annex blocks must reside within `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers not present in the analyzed AADL model.
Ensure all identifiers in AGREE contracts are visible in the target component scope.
Component type contracts should reference only visible ports and features, not implementation-specific subcomponent paths.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level bindings use assign statements in implementation scopes.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-specific subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Do not introduce new package or data type references not found in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: assume/guarantee syntax and validation repair patterns
